result.markdown:
--- title: "Get started" subtitle: "From event timings to a usable fMRI design matrix." jupyter: python3 --- ## Install ```bash pip install fmrimod ``` For local development from this checkout: ```bash pip install -e . ``` ## The 30-Second Tour Start with an event table and a sampling frame. The event table stores experimental timing; the sampling frame defines the acquired time grid. ::: {#95a2e4e9 .cell execution_count=1} ``` {.python .cell-code} import pandas as pd import fmrimod as fm from fmrimod.design_colmap import design_colmap events = pd.DataFrame({ "onset": [0, 12, 24, 36], "condition": ["face", "scene", "face", "scene"], }) sf = fm.SamplingFrame(blocklens=40, TR=1.0) ``` ::: Use a formula to turn those events into task regressors. This example creates one canonical SPM HRF regressor per condition. ::: {#489bfccb .cell execution_count=2} ``` {.python .cell-code} model = fm.event_model( 'onset ~ hrf(condition, basis="spmg1")', data=events, sampling_frame=sf, ) model.column_names ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=2} ``` ['condition_condition.face', 'condition_condition.scene'] ``` ::: ::: The resulting matrix has one row per acquired sample and one column per model regressor. ::: {#8ec56b92 .cell execution_count=3} ``` {.python .cell-code} design = model.design_matrix design.shape ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=3} ``` (40, 2) ``` ::: ::: ## What Just Happened `SamplingFrame` defines the time axis, `event_model()` parses the formula and constructs event terms, and `design_matrix` holds the convolved regressors ready for downstream GLM fitting. You can inspect the design surface with the same top-level API: ::: {#5d4f33d3 .cell execution_count=4} ``` {.python .cell-code} colmap = design_colmap(model) colmap[["col", "name", "role", "model_source"]] ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=4} ```{=html}
col name role model_source
0 1 condition_condition.face task event
1 2 condition_condition.scene task event
``` ::: ::: ## Next Steps - [Tutorial: design matrices](tutorials/design-matrices.qmd) - [API reference](reference/index.qmd)
